content.text:
You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/netobserv-web-console/source/web/src/standalone/index.css

Apply ALL of the following 3 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 254
Rule [sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-chip]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-chip' was removed in the new version. A simple version prefix swap to 'pf-v6-c-chip' does NOT produce a valid class — this class does not exist in the target CSS distribution.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a PatternFly component prop.

Incident context:
  className: pf-v5-c-chip

Code context:
```
  253  /* fix chip background colors */
  254  .pf-v5-theme-dark .pf-v5-c-chip {
  255    --pf-v5-c-chip--BackgroundColor: var(--pf-global--BackgroundColor--100);

```

### Fix 2
Line: 254
Rule [sd-css-removed-chip]:
This CSS references the 'pf-c-chip' component class which was removed in PatternFly v6.

The Chip component was rebuilt and no longer uses this CSS class. This CSS override is dead and should be removed.

Check if the behavior you were overriding is now available via a component prop instead.

Incident context:
  className: pf-v5-c-chip

Code context:
```
  253  /* fix chip background colors */
  254  .pf-v5-theme-dark .pf-v5-c-chip {
  255    --pf-v5-c-chip--BackgroundColor: var(--pf-global--BackgroundColor--100);

```

### Fix 3
Line: 254
Rule [semver-css-class-removed-c-chip]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-chip' has no equivalent in the new version. There is no valid 'pf-v6-c-chip' class — this class was removed, not renamed.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a component prop.

Incident context:
  className: pf-v5-c-chip

Code context:
```
  253  /* fix chip background colors */
  254  .pf-v5-theme-dark .pf-v5-c-chip {
  255    --pf-v5-c-chip--BackgroundColor: var(--pf-global--BackgroundColor--100);

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/netobserv-web-console/source/web/src/standalone/index.css
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.

I'll analyze the three fixes first. All three target the same code block at line 254 - they all identify that the `pf-v5-c-chip` CSS class was removed in PatternFly v6 and the CSS rule block using it is dead code that should be removed.

Let me read the file to understand the full context of the CSS rule block that needs to be removed.
Now let me check more context around this area to understand the full picture:

I can see the full picture. All three fixes target the same CSS rule block (lines 253-258). The `pf-v5-c-chip` class was removed in PatternFly v6 — there is no equivalent `pf-v6-c-chip` class. The entire rule block is dead code. I also notice that on line 254, it appears a previous migration pass already changed `.pf-v5-theme-dark` to `.pf-v6-theme-dark`, but the `pf-v5-c-chip` selector and the CSS variables inside it (which also reference removed chip variables) are all dead.

The fix is to remove the comment and the entire rule block (lines 253-258).

Let me also check if the CSS variables referenced inside are used elsewhere in this file:

The chip references only appear in this one rule block. The entire block is dead code since:

1. The selector `.pf-v5-c-chip` no longer matches anything (Chip component was removed/rebuilt in PF v6)
2. The CSS custom properties inside (`--pf-v6-c-chip--*`) are also dead — there's no `pf-v6-c-chip` class in PF v6
3. The `--pf-global--BackgroundColor--100` and `--pf-global--BorderColor--100` variables are also v5-era globals

I'll remove the comment and the entire rule block (lines 253-258).
Let me verify the result looks clean:

The file looks clean — the dead chip rule block is removed and the surrounding code flows correctly with no orphaned blank lines or artifacts.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Detail |
|-----|------|--------|--------|
| Fix 1 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-chip` | ✅ Applied | Removed the entire dead CSS rule block (lines 253–258) targeting `.pf-v5-c-chip`, including its comment, selector, and all three CSS custom property overrides. The class `pf-v5-c-chip` was removed in PF v6 with no equivalent. |
| Fix 2 | `sd-css-removed-chip` | ✅ Applied | Same code block — the Chip component was rebuilt and no longer uses this CSS class. Removed as dead code. |
| Fix 3 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-chip` | ✅ Applied | Same code block — confirmed no `pf-v6-c-chip` replacement exists. Removed as dead code. |

All three fixes targeted the identical CSS rule block (the `.pf-v6-theme-dark .pf-v5-c-chip` rule and its comment). The block was removed in a single edit that satisfies all three fixes.
